items.title: Roger Cook flexes his power with captain's call on new minister

children.content: This week, WA Premier Roger Cook stood firm against Labor's powerful factions to get the minister he wanted. Three years in, it shows the power he wields, writes Keane Bourke.

children.content: Scarborough MP Stuart Aubrey says his office was "deliberately damaged" by a vandal on Friday night.

items.title: WA shire tells hospitality business to remove 'unsafe' roadside signs

items.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird, another WA case confirmed

children.content: Agriculture Minister Julie Collins says CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the deadly strain in a greater crested tern found in South Australia's Limestone Coast.

items.title: Postal worker charged after suspected theft of AusPost mail

children.content: Australia Post worker Hussam Kassem is charged by police with mail tampering at a Perth Airport sorting facility, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

items.title: Mark McGowan's right-hand man parachuted into plum cabinet role

items.title: Workers' pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in 30 years

items.title: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win World Cup in any fashion

children.content: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

articles.title: Quick hits: It's the Mbappé show again, as England defender cops ban

items.title: Mining company hit with record fine for neglecting vulnerable bat in WA

children.content: A Western Australian gold miner is fined more than $100,000 for failing to protect a tiny, orange-furred species of bat in the state's Pilbara region.
